title: No Luck In Love.
content:
I do love I know not what,
    Sometimes this and sometimes that;
    All conditions I aim at.

    But, as luckless, I have yet
    Many shrewd disasters met
    To gain her whom I would get.

    Therefore now I'll love no more
    As I've doted heretofore:
    He who must be, shall be poor.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "No Luck In Love."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
